title: Here’s why a trade for Brian Thomas Jr. would be a home run move for the Patriots
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Brian Thomas Jr. is a potential trade target for New England Patriots due to his cost-effective contract and high upside.
Despite a down year in 2025, Thomas previously achieved over 1,300 scrimmage yards and 10 touchdowns in 2024.
Trading for Thomas would allow the Patriots financial flexibility to strengthen other positions like tight end and pass rusher.

title: ‘SNL’ recap: Best moments from Ryan Gosling hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Ryan Gosling hosted 'Saturday Night Live' on March 7, marking his fourth hosting appearance.
Musical guest Gorillaz performed their first-ever single, 'Clint Eastwood,' celebrating their 25th anniversary.
Upcoming guest Harry Styles attended, previewing his dual role as host and musical guest next week.

title: Crew member on sunken vessel off Cape Cod has died, officials identify him
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Angel Nieves, 37, from New Bedford, was found deceased after the Yankee Rose capsized off Provincetown, Cape Cod.
The Yankee Rose fishing vessel was capsized and located about two nautical miles off Provincetown, officials reported.
The search for the missing second crew member of the capsized Yankee Rose was halted due to poor weather conditions.

title: Patriots reportedly among teams interested in Packers’ leading WR in free agency
author: Conor Roche
bullets: The Patriots are interested in Packers wide receiver Romeo Doubs, a top free agent this offseason, per ESPN.
Doubs recorded 55 receptions, 724 yards, and tied for team-high with six touchdowns in the last season.
New England is also linked to Colts' Alec Pierce and interested in Eagles' A.J. Brown via trade.

title: NH cold case solved 40 years after police found man’s skull in woods
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Warren Kuchinsky, missing since the mid-1970s, identified through genetic genealogy from a skull found in 1986 in Bristol, NH.
No foul play suspected in Kuchinsky's death, confirmed by DNA testing of a surviving family member.
The DNA Doe Project aided NH authorities, narrowing identification within 24 hours at a 2025 virtual retreat.

title: Boston police issue scam warning for fake parking violations
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston police warn about fake parking violation notices mimicking official documents with Massachusetts state seal and court information.
Scam notices include a QR code for payment; police advise against scanning or paying without verification through official channels.
Victims of the scam are urged to report it to police, with anonymous tips accepted via CrimeStoppers at 1-800-494-TIPS.

title: Mansfield man charged with illegally possessing ammunition, prosecutors say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Michael Kennedy, 48, of Mansfield was charged with possession of ammunition as a convicted felon, say federal prosecutors.
Investigators found firearm parts, ammunition, and a loaded pistol during a search of Kennedy's home while on supervised release.
Kennedy, previously convicted for similar offenses, faces up to 15 years in prison and a $250,000 fine if convicted.

title: Here are four under the radar Red Sox players standing out in spring training
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Braiden Ward excels with 12 stolen bases in 12 games, accounting for 50% of Boston's team steals in spring training.
Max Ferguson leads Red Sox in RBIs with seven this spring, showcasing his positional versatility despite likely returning to minors.
Tayron Guerrero impresses in bullpen with six strikeouts and one earned run in six innings, his first MLB run since 2019.

title: Once viewed as a rental, could Viktor Arvidsson be ideal ‘bridge’ forward for Bruins?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Viktor Arvidsson ranks second on the Boston Bruins in 5-on-5 goals this season with 13 goals.
Despite trade interest, the Bruins opted to retain Arvidsson due to his value and no-movement clause.
A potential three-year extension for Arvidsson might provide stability amid the Bruins' developing young talent.

title: 5 interior offensive linemen options for Patriots after Garrett Bradbury trade
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Patriots need a new left guard after trading center Garrett Bradbury to the Bears.
Isaac Seumalo, an NFL top guard, is a free agent but may demand a high salary.
David Edwards and Joel Bitonio are experienced options, while Emmanuel Pregnon is a draft prospect.
